llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: llm_facilitator is the only AI arm (GPT-4o). no_facilitation is the human-only baseline with no facilitative support.
llm_extracted.notes: p-value reported as 'p < 0.001' (inequality), so reported_p_value set to null per instructions. Effect size Cohen's d = 0.61 explicitly reported for this comparison. Mean difference Δ = 2.9 facts (AI higher). Both Level C (t-statistic) and Level E (Cohen's d) data available.
source_quotes: However, we find that LLM facilitation increased information sharing relative to all other conditions: Δ = 2.9, t(138) = 3.58, p < 0.001 for "None"; Δ = 1.58, t(139) = 2.1, p = 0.02 for "Message"; and Δ = 2.47, t(138) = 3.37, p < 0.001 for "Human"; to contextualize the observed increase in information coverage with LLM facilitation relative to no facilitation ("LLM" vs. "None"), the effect size (Cohen's d) is 0.61, indicating a moderate-to-large practical effect of LLM facilitation on information coverage.
The number of distinct facts mentioned at least once throughout a group's discussion, ranging from 0 to 30.

computed.notes: p-value reported as 'p < 0.001' (inequality), so reported_p_value set to null per instructions. Effect size Cohen's d = 0.61 explicitly reported for this comparison. Mean difference Δ = 2.9 facts (AI higher). Both Level C (t-statistic) and Level E (Cohen's d) data available.
